raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "The request 'Review this section' is broad. Should the review prioritize (A) scientific content (claims, logic, evidence) or (B) writing quality (clarity, grammar, flow)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The review should assess the clarity and coherence of the core contribution claim regarding Lyapunov-stability and ambiguity resolution.",
    "R1.2": "The review must verify the logical flow from the problem statement about ambiguity to the proposed three-block architecture as a solution.",
    "R1.3": "The review must critically evaluate whether the reported experimental results sufficiently support the broad claims of robustness and naturalness.",
    "R1.4": "The review should assess the claimed novelty of coupling the language-grounding layer to stability analysis against prior work in language-driven robotics.",
    "R1.7": "The review should consider whether the description of 'existing systems' is a fair and complete representation of the relevant literature.",
    "RX.1": "The review must note that the method's performance depends on a tuned threshold τ, and the uncertainty of this tuning process should be disclosed.",
    "RX.2": "The review must check if the conclusion that LMCR is a 'practical recipe' for 'unstructured settings' is an overclaim based on the tabletop evaluation.",
    "RX.3": "The review must be strictly limited to the provided text and not speculate about other parts of the work.",
    "RX.4": "The review should consider any potential negative consequences of a system that acts on ambiguous commands, even with a confidence threshold.",
    "RX.5": "All points raised in the review must be directly traceable to specific statements or omissions in the provided artifact."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: The request 'Review this section' is broad. Should the review prioritize (A) scientific content (claims, logic, evidence) or (B) writing quality (clarity, grammar, flow)?

rationale.R1.1: The review should assess the clarity and coherence of the core contribution claim regarding Lyapunov-stability and ambiguity resolution.
rationale.R1.2: The review must verify the logical flow from the problem statement about ambiguity to the proposed three-block architecture as a solution.
rationale.R1.3: The review must critically evaluate whether the reported experimental results sufficiently support the broad claims of robustness and naturalness.
rationale.R1.4: The review should assess the claimed novelty of coupling the language-grounding layer to stability analysis against prior work in language-driven robotics.
rationale.R1.7: The review should consider whether the description of 'existing systems' is a fair and complete representation of the relevant literature.
rationale.RX.1: The review must note that the method's performance depends on a tuned threshold τ, and the uncertainty of this tuning process should be disclosed.
rationale.RX.2: The review must check if the conclusion that LMCR is a 'practical recipe' for 'unstructured settings' is an overclaim based on the tabletop evaluation.
rationale.RX.3: The review must be strictly limited to the provided text and not speculate about other parts of the work.
rationale.RX.4: The review should consider any potential negative consequences of a system that acts on ambiguous commands, even with a confidence threshold.
rationale.RX.5: All points raised in the review must be directly traceable to specific statements or omissions in the provided artifact.
